title: Height of Mount Everest: How Tall Is The Mount Everest?
content: Himalayan Recreation Treks & Expedition Pvt.Ltd. How tall is Mount Everest?.webp). # How Tall Is Mount Everest? The height of Mount Everest is one of the most debated topics in geography and mountaineering. Individuals asked for years, "How tall is Mount Everest?" The answer has changed slightly over time due to better technology and natural reasons like earthquakes. Today, Mount Everest's height is officially 8,848.86 meters (29,031.7 feet). This new elevation of Mount Everest was approved in 2020 by Nepal and China after a joint survey. The previous widely recognized height was 8,848 meters, which was surveyed by India in 1955 and confirmed by a Chinese survey in 1975. ## What is the Official Height of Mount Everest in 2025? As of 2025, Mount Everest's official elevation is 8,848.86 meters (29,031.7 feet). To address the height concerns over Mount Everest's height, Nepal and China worked together to re-measure the mountain in a coordinated manner, using GPS and radar.

title: New Height for Everest - The Blog on alanarnette.com
content: After a few years of work, and suspense, China and Nepal jointly announced that they have an updated measurement for Mt. Everest, and both countries agree on it. The Chinese used state of the art technology, while The Trigonometric Survey used basic math and raw human skills, and their measurement was a scant 9.06 meters different – amazing! * 1955 – Survey of India Service: 29,029-feet/8,848-meters (snow cap). * 1975 – Chinese measurement of 29,029.30-feet/8848.13-meters (snow cap). * 1999 – American Everest Expedition (Bradford Washburn): 29,035-feet/8850-meters (rock head; snow/ice elevation 3-feet/1-m higher). * 2010 – Nepal and China agreed on 29,029-feet/8,848 (snow cap with rock head at 29015.9-feet/8,844-meters). * 2020 – China survey team is completed taking measurements: 8848.86 -meter/ 29,031.69291-feet. title: China, Nepal announce revised Everest height - DW News
content: The new official height is 8,848.86 meters — roughly one meter higher than Nepal's previous measurement. Global warming and a 2015 earthquake spurred speculation over the actual height of the world's tallest peak. China and Nepal announced a "revised height" for Mount Everest on Tuesday, after separately spending months measuring the world's tallest peak. That new height is 8,848.86 meters (29,032 feet), up by just one meter from Nepal's previous measurement and about four meters from China's. It was the first time Nepal had independently carried out such measurements, amid speculation over a possible change in height due to global warming and a 2015 earthquake. Nepal previously recognized 8,848 meters as Mount Everest's official snow height, which was the finding of a survey conducted by the Survey of India in the early 1950s.
